paragraphs.body: Murray Gell-Mann once said, “Both biological and cultural diversity are now severely threatened and working for their preservation is a critical task.”(Gell-Mann). Culture is something that can be hard to keep up with when you’re not with the people who always remind you about it. And because of that many people are starting to forget their own culture and have a distance of where they came from, forgetting who they truly are.

It was a hot humid summer in Korea. I was walking around a small alley way that had a lot of small, cute stores with my mom. And my mom and I walked into this one store that had a lot of cute and fancy figurines that we like to collect and interesting looking magnets to stick on our fridge. We walked around the store for a little bit and came across a whole keychain section. The whole wall was filled with so many keychains, and they weren’t like the same keychains there were a variety of them. I’ve never seen this many keychains on a wall. All the keychains were different from each other that every time I looked over at a different section of the wall it was just a whole new world of keychains. Some of them would be goofy with silly faces or some cute characters that was popular at that time. But out of all these keychains my mom specifically chose one that represents Korea, because she wants me to be proud of who I am. This keychain was a little pink and yellow sac with a gold pig and a Korean flag on it. Many people used to wear them back in the day as a good luck charm but without the flag. My mom got this keychain for me so that I have some luck while I’m in America away from her. She wanted to support me in some way that she got me this little keychain that I could carry around easily anywhere I wanted to, to make it seem like she’s always there next to me.

annotations.text: But out of all these keychains my mom specifically chose one that represents Korea, because she wants me to be proud of who I am. This keychain was a little pink and yellow sac with a gold pig and a Korean flag on it.
annotations.comment: Nice. You're already revealing the metaphor of the object!
What if you started your essay here? The ideas in your current intro will play out more strongly in-text, I bet :)

paragraphs.body: This keychain that my mom gifted me is a very special gift that I can never forget about it. It would be light as a feather except for the Korean flag. The Korean flag was the only part that gave the extra weight to the key chain. Every time you hold the sac you can feel the soft luxury cotton rubbing against your hand feeling like a mini pillow, but at the same time you would feel the coldness rubbing against you when the gold pig swung by. Even though it felt luxurious when it touched me, I would get a whiff of the cheap factory smell passing right by me. The small gold pig painted in fake gold paint with a cover of high-gloss paint to make it look exquisite, radiating that strong smell of paint and having that bit of plastic texture. The sac filled with cotton having strings of thread popping out between the pink and yellow patterns with all the cotton poking out looking like it could fall apart any moment, but the Korean flag was staying strong and sturdy with no sight of paint chipped off, none of that strong chemical paint smell was radiating off it, only the smell of metal. I can vividly see the red and blue colors on the core of the flag with the black stripes in the corners perfectly projecting the Korean flag, outlined with gold all around it. No matter what it bumps into it would stay strong with no sight of paint chipping off or indents.

annotations.context_left: that gave the extra weight to the key chain. Every time you hold the sac you can feel the soft

paragraphs.body: This was when I was entering middle school, and my parents had to permanently stay in Korea. I won’t have the warm Korean food my mom would have ready for me after school or even just breakfast, lunch and dinner. I won’t have my dad who would always cook late night snacks for us before we watched Korean shows and drama in the living room together. I have to eat all the American greasy food served now. I have to watch Korean shows all by myself. If I want to eat warm Korean food, I have to learn how to cook it or even just look for restaurants that will cook some up for me. And now I was about to lose the two people who always reminded me of who I am deep inside. I was about to have a whole independent life by myself in America with my older siblings only. And because I won’t have my parents who will remind me of my culture, she wanted to give me a small little gift so that I don’t forget who I am. Even if the material felt cheap all that mattered to me was that the Korean flag was the sturdiest part of the keychain. I have something I can represent to the world of who I am.

annotations.text: all that mattered to me was that the Korean flag was the sturdiest part of the keychain.
annotations.comment: Yes! You're deepening the metaphor, here. Neat. I wonder, though, _why_ your Korean side might be the sturdiest, most important part of you? Is it?

paragraphs.body: When I went to school, everybody always asked if I was Japanese or Chinese. They never asked if I was Korean. That’s when I was able to confidently show my keychain with the Korean flag on it. I knew if my mom was next to me, she would be proud of me this moment because I’m not forgetting about who I am and where I come from. If I didn’t have this keychain by my side, I might’ve felt embarrassed and not be able to speak up on who I am. And by this keychain always sticking next to me, it gave me confidence on who I should be and constantly reminding me of my culture and allowing me to make some Korean friends who would also always remind me of where I come from. Whenever I go over to my friends home their parents would happily greet me and share the food, they cooked up with me. Thinking back at this moment I’m thankful that my mom got me this keychain because if I didn’t maybe I would’ve forgotten my own culture and not know anything about it. But even till this day I’m happy that my culture is stuck with me, and I can be proud of who I am. I think if my mom never gifted me this keychain, I would’ve felt very disappointed in myself for not staying connected to my culture and possibly lose a part of myself. And because Korea is more well known today, I’m proud to say that I’m Korean and that I know their culture and know how to speak Korean.

annotations.text: I’m not forgetting about who I am and where I come from. If I didn’t have this keychain by my side, I might’ve felt embarrassed and not be able to speak up on who I am.
annotations.comment: Why are you proud of being Korean? What does that mean to you? Let's talk :)

paragraphs.body: It's important to keep close contact with the culture you grew up in because it is a thankful thing to have in your life and getting to know better about where you come from and not be embarrassed about who you are. Instead of being a part of the threatened group of cultural diversity it’s better to stay connected and be proud of who you are. By you staying connected to your culture it can allow you to spread your culture even more and maybe even teach those who lost their culture.

annotations.text: Instead of being a part of the threatened group of cultural diversity it’s better to stay connected and be proud of who you are.
